result.summary: Dataflow on dataflow.googleapis.com exposes a Google Cloud REST API, a public MCP endpoint at `/mcp`, and the `gcloud dataflow` CLI.
result.description: Google Cloud Dataflow is a managed service for running batch and streaming data processing pipelines. It lets users launch, inspect, and manage Dataflow jobs and templates on Google Cloud.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token / Application Default Credentials
gcp_oauth.setup: For server-to-server or local development, set up Google Cloud credentials using [Application Default Credentials](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/provide-credentials-adc) and the REST auth guide at [Authenticate for using REST](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/rest). For local user credentials, the easiest path is usually `gcloud auth application-default login` or `gcloud auth login` after installing the [gcloud CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install-sdk).

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key
gcp_api_key.setup: Create and manage an API key from the Google Cloud Console following [Manage API keys](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys). Google APIs commonly accept the key as the `key` query parameter; restrict the key appropriately per [Best practices for managing API keys](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys-best-practices).

variables.name: region
variables.resolveFrom: choose one of the regional endpoints listed in the discovery document when using regional hostnames
variables.description: Optional regional endpoint location such as `us-central1`; discovery lists hosts like `https://dataflow.{region}.rep.googleapis.com/`.

evidence: authoritative seed fact: MCP server endpoint https://dataflow.googleapis.com/mcp (auth: none)
